ps140: Rejoice, O most wise Elisha,* for having purified your mind* from bodily passions,* you received spiritual dawn, O glorious one,* which you transmitted to all others,* and appeared wholly light-like.* Therefore, you have made your dwelling with the never-setting Light.* Pray always for us* who praise you.

Elijah the zealot* left you a double portion of grace* and enlightened you, O blessed one,* he who proved himself a heavenly warrior.* Following his example,* by grace you halted the rushing torrent of the river* and crossed over, O glorious one,* joyfully adorning yourself* and magnifying Christ.

Of old, your prayer with faith* gave a child to the Shunammite woman,* and you raised him from the dead* as a divine prophet, adorned with holy miracles.* And again, through salt* you transformed barren water into fruitfulness.* You work countless miracles* through the grace of the all-creative Spirit:* therefore we hymn you, O blessed one.

O divinely wise father Methodius,* like a lion, by the roar of your most wise teachings* you terrified the sly fox John,* for he could not endure your denunciation,* and the impious one was struck down by a sword-wound.* Therefore, as a champion of the Orthodox people,* you did not allow the flock of Christ* to perish.

O firmament of Orthodoxy,* shepherd of the Church,* adornment of monks,* river overflowing with the waters of the Spirit,* cup pouring forth the sweetest drink,* luminary shining to the ends of the world,* double-edged sword sharpened by grace,* cutting down the armies of the ungodly - ** that is who you are, O Methodius!

A model of confession,* foundation of holy hierarchs,* instrument inspired by God,* playing teachings full of piety,* house of wisdom and abyss of love,* ever-flowing stream of mercy,* bowl pouring forth the drink of compunction,* flourishing garden of paradise - ** that is who you are, O Methodius!

The divine coming of the prophets has shone forth today,* mystically calling out to the souls of the lovers of the feasts.* He who mounted the chariot and crossed the heavens,* the star of the never-waning East,* together with Elisha, the man of wonders,* broadens the streams of the new Jordan,* making clear the proclamation of piety* and manifestly portraying the double blessing* by a twofold image of the unison glory of the Old and the New** for those who celebrate their most festive memorial with faith.
stavrotheotokion: Beholding You nailed to the Tree of the Cross, O Jesus,* weeping, she that knew not wedlock said:* O sweet Child, have You therefore abandoned me who alone gave birth to You,* O unapproachable Light of the all-unoriginate Father?* Make haste and glorify yourself* that they that glorify your divine sufferings may receive divine glory!
additional_aposticha: The Church of God is clothed today in gladness* and rejoices, crying aloud:* “My beauty has shone forth beyond any city!* For behold, the glorious Methodius, the great treasure of holy hierarchs,* has passed over to the heavens!* Come, therefore, O lovers of the feasts,* assembly of the Orthodox faithful,* let us form a choir around his holy reliquary,* receiving streams of healing from it,** pray to Christ God that He may deliver the whole world from all heresy!”

O prophet and preacher of Christ,* you never depart from the throne of majesty,* and you always intercede for all who are sick.* Ministering on high, you bless the whole world.* While accepting honours from everywhere,** ask for cleansing of our souls.
aposticha_verses: Your priests shall be clothed with righteousness,* and Your righteous shall rejoice.
stavrotheotokion_aposticha: Beholding our Life hanging upon the Tree,* the most immaculate Theotokos cried out, lamenting maternally:* “O my Son and my God, save those who hymn You with love!”
